content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Mark Stephenson 
316 17th Street 
Pacific Grove, California 93950 
RE: Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-11-104 
Dear Mr. Stephenson: 
July 12, 2011 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the post-governmental 
employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 This letter is based on the facts 
presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder 
of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) 
Additionally, our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. 'We therefore offer 
no opinion on the application, if any, of other post-governmental employment laws such as 
Public Contract Code Section l 0411. 
QUESTION 
May you, after retiring from the State of California Department of Fish and Game, work 
for the University of California, Davis or California State University, San Jose performing 
environmental research work on contracts with other state agencies? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. The post-governmental employment provisions neither prohibit you from working 
on contracts that you were not involved in while working for the state nor from contacting other 
state agencies where you were non employed. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No.A-11-104 
Page No. 2 
You currently work for the Department of Fish and Game ("DFG") and are involved in 
environmental research work. Your position is designated in the agency's conflict of interest 
code. There are multiple agencies in the State of California besides DFG that issue contracts to 
do environmental research to California State University, San Jose (CSUSJ) or the University of 
California, Davis (UCD). You have been directly involved in a few contracts between DFG and 
SJSU, but you have not been involved in other contracts from other departments and agencies 
with these universities. 
You would like to retire and work for one of these universities doing environmental 
research. You understand that you cannot receive any funds from contracts that you were 
involved in while working for DFG. You seek advice as to whether you may work on other 
environmentally-oriented contracts from other state agencies and departments. You have two 
options: (1) work half-time as a retired annuitant for DFG and the other half for one of the 
universities, or (2) work full time after retirement for one of the universities. You would like to 
work on contracts received from agencies other than DFG. 
Last year, you sought advice with respect to the post-governmental provisions of the Act 
(Stephenson Advice Letter, No. I-10-127) in which we provided you informal assistance with 
respect to those provisions. Because the assistance provided in that letter is still applicable, we 
will not repeat it here. Our analysis is, therefore, limited to your specific question as to whether 
you may perform environmental research work on contacts between your potential employer 
(either CSUSJ or UCD) and any other state agency other that DFG. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act's "permanent ban" does not apply because you will not be not be acting "as 
agent or attorney for ... any person ( other than the State of California) before any court or state 
administrative agency ... by making any formal or informal appearance, or by making any oral 
or written communication with the intent to influence, in connection with any judicial, quasi­
judicial or other proceeding" in which the State of California is a party and in which proceeding 
you participated while employed by DFG. We assume you will not aid, advise, counsel, consult, 
or assist in representing any person in any proceeding you were involved in as well. 
The Act's "one-year ban" will also not apply so long as you do not represent any other 
person, for compensation "by making any formal or informal appearance, or by making any oral 
or written communication, before [DFG for 12 month after you left your position], if the 
appearance or communication is made for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative 
action, or influencing any action or proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or 
revocation of a permit, license, grant, or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property." 


File No.A-11-104 
Page No. 3 
Because you will be doing research work for a public university of the state and will be 
working on contracts with other agencies than the one for which you were formerly employed, 
the Act's post-governmental employment provisions will not impact this work. 
If you continue to work for DFG on a part-time basis as a retired annuitant while working 
on research work with one of the universities for contracts not involving DFG, you should 
consult your agency regarding its incompatible activities policy and be aware of the conflict of 
interest laws in any decisions in which you may be involved that could have a financial effect on 
your university employer. 
Sincerely, 
John W. Wallace 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
WJL:jgl

May you, after retiring from the State of California Department of Fish and Game, work 
for the University of California, Davis or California State University, San Jose performing 
environmental research work on contracts with other state agencies?

state agencies where you were non employed.

sections.analysis:
The Act's "permanent ban" does not apply because you will not be not be acting "as

Because you will be doing research work for a public university of the state and will be

If you continue to work for DFG on a part-time basis as a retired annuitant while working

consult your agency regarding its incompatible activities policy and be aware of the conflict of 
interest laws in any decisions in which you may be involved that could have a financial effect on 
your university employer.

QUESTION: May you, after retiring from the State of California Department of Fish and Game, work 
for the University of California, Davis or California State University, San Jose performing 
environmental research work on contracts with other state agencies?

CONCLUSION: Yes. The post-governmental employment provisions neither prohibit you from working 
on contracts that you were not involved in while working for the state nor from contacting other 
state agencies where you were non employed.
